name: El Toboso — circular from Campo de Criptana
name_original: El Toboso — circular desde Campo de Criptana
region_intro: Touring cycling around Campo De Criptana offers routes through the distinctive landscapes of Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. The region is characterized by the Sierra de los Molinos, a small elevation featuring historic windmills, and extends into the vast, arid La Mancha plains. Cyclists can also encounter riparian areas along the River Záncara and the wetlands of Laguna de la Veguilla, part of the San Juan Lagoon Complex. This varied terrain provides a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with routes ranging from relatively flat to those with significant elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Campo De Criptana The most popular touring cycling route is El Toboso — circular from Campo de Criptana, a 35.3 miles (56.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 58 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects Campo de Criptana with the historic town of El Toboso, offering expansive views of the La Mancha plains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ermita de San Antón – Río Záncara loop from Campo de Criptana, a moderate 35.6 miles (57.3 km) path. This route explores the area around the Hermitage of San Antón and follows the picturesque River Záncara, providing a mix of cultural and natural scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Hermitage of Cristo de Villajos – Campo de Criptana Windmills loop from Campo de Criptana, a 21.9 miles (35.3 km) trail leading through the iconic Sierra de los Molinos, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Touring cycling around Campo De Criptana is defined by its historic windmills, arid plains, and occasional wetlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Campo De Criptana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 8 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Campo De Criptana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Campo de Criptana?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Campo de Criptana, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and interests. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the touring cycling routes in Campo de Criptana?
A: The routes range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 9 moderate routes and 14 difficult routes, ensuring options for both experienced cyclists and those looking for a challenge. There is also one easy route available for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Campo de Criptana?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Campo de Criptana are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056700273/el-toboso-circular-from-campo-de-criptana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Toboso — circular from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt; route and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Antón – Río Záncara loop from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain around Campo de Criptana is varied. You'll encounter the distinctive arid plains of La Mancha, sections along the picturesque River Záncara, and the small elevation of the Sierra de los Molinos. Routes often feature a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with some offering significant elevation changes.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I see along the routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views, particularly from the Sierra de los Molinos, which is home to the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2909432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3082656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Cristo de Villajos&lt;/a&gt; or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt;, a wetland area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24532746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Cristo de Villajos – Campo de Criptana Windmills loop from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for seeing several landmarks.

Q: Are there any natural features to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt;, part of the San Juan Lagoon Complex, which is a crucial habitat for migratory birds and offers bird observation opportunities. Some routes also follow the scenic River Záncara, providing riparian views.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while touring cycling?
A: Yes, particularly around the wetland areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt; is known for its migratory birds, making it an excellent spot for nature observation. Keep an eye out for various bird species as you cycle through these natural habitats.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Campo de Criptana?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling in Campo de Criptana, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot in La Mancha, so early mornings or late afternoons are advisable if cycling during this season. Winters are typically mild but can be chilly.

Q: Are there options for families or beginners?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is one easy route available, which could be suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. It's always recommended to check the route details, including distance and elevation, to ensure it matches your group's fitness level.

Q: What are the typical durations for touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24532746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Cristo de Villajos – Campo de Criptana Windmills loop from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes, while longer routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056700273/el-toboso-circular-from-campo-de-criptana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Toboso — circular from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt; may take closer to 5 hours.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites to visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: Campo de Criptana is rich in history. Beyond the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2909432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt;, you can explore the 16th-century Pósito (Granary), various hermitages like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3082656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Cristo de Villajos&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique Albaicín neighborhood with its Moorish-influenced architecture. Some windmills also house museums dedicated to local history and culture.

Q: What makes touring cycling in Campo de Criptana unique?
A: The region's unique blend of cultural heritage and distinctive landscapes sets it apart. Cycling through the vast La Mancha plains with the iconic windmills in the backdrop, famously associated with Don Quixote, provides a truly immersive and historical experience that combines natural beauty with literary charm.
tips: The old ones, Sardinero, Burleta and Infante; Sovereigns of time and stories, they have been standing since the 16th century, preserving their original mechanism. The moderns, the Inca Garcilaso, the Cariari, the Vicente Huidobro, the Pilón, the Lagarto, the Culebro and the Poyatos are immense young men from 1900.

The Hermitage or Sanctuary of the Holy Christ of Villajos is one of the most emblematic places in Campo de Criptana (Ciudad Real).
It originated from the old village of Villajos, which was abandoned in the 14th century. The original church was dedicated to Our Lady of Villajos.

It can be said that the town of El Toboso is already recognized worldwide and its fame is due to the fact that according to the famous Cervantine work, "Dulcinea" lived here or better known as Aldonza Lorenzo, who was the lady of Don Quixote.
The town is very picturesque and its history not only revolves around the famous Quixote, it has also been of great importance in the history of Spain, being the place where the battle of Navas de Tolosa was fought in 1212 and later it will form part of the domain of the Order of Santiago.
Undoubtedly a town worth visiting with the whole family.
